Claude.notes: The module is conceptually strong with well-chosen real-world cases, consistent narrative framing, and clear progression across four lessons that genuinely build capability. The critical weakness is Lab executability: the chat labs are rendered as UI shells with send/reset buttons but no backend integration code is visible in the HTML — there is no API call, no system prompt initialization, and no JavaScript function body for chatSend() or chatReset(), meaning the labs as delivered are non-functional and learners cannot complete the hands-on activities. The quiz logic (answer() function) also has no visible implementation, though the feedback markup exists, suggesting both are dependent on external JS not included in the evaluated artifact.
Claude.topIssue: Labs and quizzes are non-functional as delivered: chatSend(), chatReset(), answer(), and goPage() JavaScript functions are absent from the HTML, meaning all interactive components — the primary applied-outcome vehicle — will fail at runtime. Functional backend wiring for the chat labs and quiz answer handling must be implemented before the module can meet its stated learning outcomes.

Perplexity.notes: Module demonstrates strong narrative framing, accurate concept coverage, and appropriate cognitive calibration across three well-structured lessons. Perplexity.topIssue: Lab executability (D5-C1) is the critical failure. The three labs lack: (1) concrete deliverables learners must produce, (2) clear success criteria or rubrics, (3) any mechanism to verify the AI coach's responses are pedagogically sound or aligned to lesson concepts, (4) scaffolding or worked examples. Labs must shift from open-ended chat to structured problem-solving with defined outputs (e.g., 'produce a 3-component interview rubric,' 'draft a 90-day communication timeline with specific dates and audiences,' 'identify and document three concrete interventions'). Without this, learners cannot demonstrate the capability delta promised by the module.
